title: Warren Buffett Explained Why Netflix's AI Edge Won't Last -- 40 Years Ago
excerpt: Netflix produced a scene 10 times faster using generative AI tools, but any advantage will be fleeting.
content_html:
Streaming giant Netflix (NASDAQ: NFLX) failed to meet expectations with its second-quarter results and guidance last week, but the company did mark an important first. A sequence from Argentine science fiction series and Netflix original El Eternauta used generative AI for a building collapse scene. This is the debut of final footage created with generative AI tools appearing in a Netflix original film or series.

Netflix co-CEO Ted Sarandos noted in the earnings call that it would have taken ten times as long to create the sequence using traditional tools and workflows. He framed the AI tools as a way for creators to make better content for less money.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

The prospect of dramatically shorter production times and lower costs for Netflix's originals sounds great on the surface, but an old Warren Buffett quote throws some cold water on the idea that this will give Netflix a durable cost advantage.

Standing on your tiptoes at a parade

In his 1985 letter to shareholders, Warren Buffett laid out the trap that companies can fall into in their quest to lower costs. Buffett was talking about textile mills, a commodity business that is very different from streaming. But the lesson still applies.

Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway tried for years to make its textile business work, turning to capital expenditures that promised to lower variable costs. Buffett noted that each investment made sense in a vacuum and was backed by sound logic, but that they delivered no benefits. What was the problem? Everyone else in the textile business followed suit. "Many of our competitors, both domestic and foreign, were stepping up to the same kind of expenditures and, once enough companies did so, their reduced costs became the baseline for reduced prices industrywide," Buffett said.

Buffett likened the situation to everyone standing on their tiptoes at a parade. If everyone does it, no one sees any better, and everyone is a little more uncomfortable. Generative AI for content creation fits this mold. Netflix is seeing faster production times and lower costs, but so will all its competitors. No one will be willing to pay more for higher-quality content because the bar for quality will be raised. And, perhaps worse for Netflix, the barrier to entry into the content creation business will collapse.

sentences.zh: 问题出在哪里？

sentences.en: Everyone else in the textile business followed suit.
sentences.zh: 纺织行业的其他人也纷纷效仿。

sentences.en: "Many of our competitors, both domestic and foreign, were stepping up to the same kind of expenditures and, once enough companies did so, their reduced costs became the baseline for reduced prices industrywide," Buffett said.
sentences.zh: 巴菲特表示：“我们的许多竞争对手，无论是国内还是国外，都在加大同样的支出，一旦有足够多的公司这样做，它们降低的成本就成为全行业降价的基准。”

No one will be willing to pay more for higher-quality content because the bar for quality will be raised. And, perhaps worse for Netflix, the barrier to entry into the content creation business will collapse.

sentences.zh: 巴菲特将这种情况比作游行中每个人都踮起脚尖。

sentences.en: If everyone does it, no one sees any better, and everyone is a little more uncomfortable.
sentences.zh: 如果每个人都这样做，没有人看得更好，而且每个人都会有点不舒服。

sentences.en: Generative AI for content creation fits this mold.
sentences.zh: 用于内容创建的生成式人工智能适合这种模式。

sentences.en: Netflix is seeing faster production times and lower costs, but so will all its competitors.
sentences.zh: Netflix 的制作时间越来越短，成本也越来越低，所有竞争对手也将如此。

sentences.en: No one will be willing to pay more for higher-quality content because the bar for quality will be raised.
sentences.zh: 没有人愿意为更高质量的内容支付更多费用，因为质量的门槛将会提高。

sentences.en: And, perhaps worse for Netflix, the barrier to entry into the content creation business will collapse.
sentences.zh: 而且，对于 Netflix 来说也许更糟的是，进入内容创作业务的壁垒将会崩溃。

content_html_zh:
流媒体巨头 Netflix（纳斯达克股票代码：NFLX）上周发布的第二季度业绩和指引未能达到预期，但该公司确实创下了重要的第一。 阿根廷科幻小说系列和 Netflix 原创《El Eternauta》中的一个片段使用生成式 AI 来呈现建筑物倒塌场景。 这是使用生成式 AI 工具创建的最终镜头首次出现在 Netflix 原创电影或剧集中。

Netflix 联合首席执行官特德·萨兰多斯 (Ted Sarandos) 在财报电话会议上指出，使用传统工具和工作流程创建序列需要十倍的时间。 他将人工智能工具定义为创作者以更少的钱制作更好的内容的一种方式。

错过了 2009 年的 Nvidia？ 这个罕见的信号再次闪烁。 2009年，一家名不见经传的芯片制造商英伟达发出了“双倍下跌”的信号。 多年来，对于一家规模仅为 Nvidia 1/100 的公司来说，同样的“完全确信”信号闪烁。 继续 ”

Netflix 原创作品的制作时间大幅缩短和成本降低的前景表面上听起来不错，但沃伦·巴菲特的一句老话给 Netflix 带来持久成本优势的想法泼了一盆冷水。

在游行中踮起脚尖

沃伦·巴菲特在 1985 年致股东的信中指出了公司在寻求降低成本时可能陷入的陷阱。 巴菲特谈论的是纺织厂，这是一种与流媒体截然不同的大宗商品业务。 但教训仍然适用。

巴菲特的伯克希尔哈撒韦公司多年来一直试图让其纺织业务运转起来，转向承诺降低可变成本的资本支出。 巴菲特指出，每项投资在真空中都是有意义的，并且都有合理的逻辑支持，但它们没有带来任何好处。 问题出在哪里？ 纺织行业的其他人也纷纷效仿。 巴菲特表示：“我们的许多竞争对手，无论是国内还是国外，都在加大同样的支出，一旦有足够多的公司这样做，它们降低的成本就成为全行业降价的基准。”

巴菲特将这种情况比作游行中每个人都踮起脚尖。 如果每个人都这样做，没有人看得更好，而且每个人都会有点不舒服。 用于内容创建的生成式人工智能适合这种模式。 Netflix 的制作时间越来越短，成本也越来越低，所有竞争对手也将如此。 没有人愿意为更高质量的内容支付更多费用，因为质量的门槛将会提高。 而且，对于 Netflix 来说也许更糟的是，进入内容创作业务的壁垒将会崩溃。
